cards.front: What is a chemical bond?
cards.back: A force that holds atoms together in a compound

cards.front: Why do atoms form chemical bonds?
cards.back: To achieve a stable electron configuration (usually octet)

cards.front: What is the octet rule?
cards.back: Atoms tend to gain, lose, or share electrons to achieve 8 valence electrons

cards.front: What type of bond involves the transfer of electrons?
cards.back: Ionic bond

cards.front: What type of bond involves the sharing of electrons?
cards.back: Covalent bond

cards.front: What type of bond involves a sea of delocalized electrons?
cards.back: Metallic bond

cards.front: What types of elements typically form ionic bonds?
cards.back: Metals and nonmetals

cards.front: What types of elements typically form covalent bonds?
cards.back: Nonmetals with nonmetals

cards.front: What is a cation?
cards.back: A positively charged ion (metal loses electrons)

cards.front: What is an anion?
cards.back: A negatively charged ion (nonmetal gains electrons)

cards.front: What is an ionic compound called?
cards.back: Salt or crystal lattice

cards.front: What are properties of ionic compounds?
cards.back: High melting points, conduct electricity when molten/dissolved, brittle

cards.front: What is a single covalent bond?
cards.back: One pair of electrons shared between two atoms

cards.front: What is a double covalent bond?
cards.back: Two pairs of electrons shared between two atoms

cards.front: What is a triple covalent bond?
cards.back: Three pairs of electrons shared between two atoms

cards.front: What is a polar covalent bond?
cards.back: Electrons are shared unequally between atoms

cards.front: What is a nonpolar covalent bond?
cards.back: Electrons are shared equally between atoms

cards.front: What is electronegativity?
cards.back: The ability of an atom to attract electrons in a bond

cards.front: What electronegativity difference indicates an ionic bond?
cards.back: Greater than 1.7

cards.front: What electronegativity difference indicates a polar covalent bond?
cards.back: Between 0.4 and 1.7

cards.front: What electronegativity difference indicates a nonpolar covalent bond?
cards.back: Less than 0.4

cards.front: What is a molecule?
cards.back: A group of atoms held together by covalent bonds

cards.front: What is a diatomic molecule?
cards.back: A molecule composed of two atoms

cards.front: Name the seven diatomic elements.
cards.back: H₂, N₂, O₂, F₂, Cl₂, Br₂, I₂

cards.front: What is a Lewis dot structure?
cards.back: A diagram showing valence electrons as dots around an element symbol

cards.front: What is a structural formula?
cards.back: A formula showing the arrangement of atoms and bonds in a molecule

cards.front: What is VSEPR theory?
cards.back: Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion theory predicts molecular shapes

cards.front: What is the shape of a molecule with 2 electron pairs?
cards.back: Linear

cards.front: What is the shape of a molecule with 3 electron pairs?
cards.back: Trigonal planar

cards.front: What is the shape of a molecule with 4 electron pairs?
cards.back: Tetrahedral

cards.front: What is the shape of a water molecule (H₂O)?
cards.back: Bent or V-shaped

cards.front: What is the shape of an ammonia molecule (NH₃)?
cards.back: Trigonal pyramidal

cards.front: What is the shape of a methane molecule (CH₄)?
cards.back: Tetrahedral

cards.front: What is an intermolecular force?
cards.back: Attraction between molecules

cards.front: What is the strongest intermolecular force?
cards.back: Hydrogen bonding

cards.front: What is required for hydrogen bonding to occur?
cards.back: H bonded to N, O, or F

cards.front: What are properties of covalent compounds?
cards.back: Low melting/boiling points, poor conductors

cards.front: What is a metallic bond?
cards.back: Attraction between metal cations and delocalized electrons

cards.front: What are properties of metals?
cards.back: Malleable, ductile, conduct electricity, lustrous

cards.front: What is an alloy?
cards.back: A mixture of a metal with other elements

cards.front: Give an example of an alloy.
cards.back: Steel, brass, bronze
